You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. If you are unsure or have assumptions, you prefer to test them yourself or ask questions to clarify requirements.
General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-assistant/agent --branch issue-131-e0fcd2d405df --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-assistant/agent --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an examples and/or experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, use WebFetch tool (or fetch tool) to download the image first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's HTML 404 pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and may crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\" or \"text\", the download failed and you should retry or skip the image.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/131.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-assistant [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/pulls/132/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/issues/132/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/pulls/132/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/issues/131/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.
   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 132 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo link-assistant/agent --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 132, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, ALWAYS run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,
      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request answer to original requirements of the issue,
      make sure no new new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 132.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-131-e0fcd2d405df.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-131-e0fcd2d405df. (Note: PR 132 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.

   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
   - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
   - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
   - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
   - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
   - When you finish using the browser, always close it with browser_close to free resources." | jq -c .)

General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-assistant/agent --branch issue-131-e0fcd2d405df --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-assistant/agent --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says "failing" but tools show "passing", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an examples and/or experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's HTML 404 pages saved as .png) can cause "Could not process image" errors and may crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows "HTML" or "text", the download failed and you should retry or skip the image.

  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01NyejgAbmxRe3s4cqEBnJHr",
        "type": "tool_result",
        "content": "title:\tdocs: case study for --tool agent failure (issue #1151)\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tkonard\nlabels:\t\nassignees:\tkonard\nreviewers:\t\nprojects:\t\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t1163\nurl:\thttps://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1163\nadditions:\t569\ndeletions:\t1\nauto-merge:\tdisabled\n--\n## Summary\n\nThis PR addresses issue #1151 (`--tool agent` does not work) with both documentation and a fix for the `--verbose` mode.\n\n### Issues Addressed\n\n1. **Primary Issue: ZodError crashes** - This was in the `@link-assistant/agent` package, fixed in v0.8.6+\n2. **Secondary Issue: --verbose flag not working** - Fixed in this PR\n\n### Root Cause Analysis\n\n#### Primary Issue (ZodError)\n\nThe issue was **NOT** in hive-mind's `solve.mjs` but in the `@link-assistant/agent` package version 0.8.5.\n\n**Problem:** When using Bun 1.3.6+ with AI SDK 6.0.0-beta.99, the SDK returns:\n- Token counts as objects: `{ total: 8707, noCache: 6339, cacheRead: 2368 }` instead of numbers\n- finishReason as objects: `{ type: \"stop\" }` instead of strings\n\nThis caused ZodError crashes because the agent code expected primitives.\n\n**Resolution:** Fixed in `@link-assistant/agent` v0.8.6+ (PR #126)\n\n#### Secondary Issue (--verbose not working)\n\n**Problem:** The `@link-assistant/agent` CLI sends **ALL output to stderr** (not stdout), including:\n- Verbose log messages: `{\"log\":{\"level\":\"info\",...}}`\n- Structured events: `{\"type\":\"step_start\",...}`, `{\"type\":\"tool_use\",...}`, etc.\n\nThe original hive-mind `agent.lib.mjs` code processed stdout with JSON parsing and formatting, but treated stderr as plain error text without parsing. This caused verbose logs to not be displayed properly.\n\n### Changes in this PR\n\n1. **Fixed stderr handling in `src/agent.lib.mjs`:**\n   - Process stderr the same way as stdout (NDJSON line-by-line parsing)\n   - Format JSON output nicely with indentation\n   - Extract session IDs from stderr messages\n   - Collect stderr output for error detection\n\n2. **Added case study documentation at `docs/case-studies/issue-1151/`:**\n   - Root cause analysis\n   - Investigation findings for --verbose issue\n   - Timeline of events\n   - Lessons learned\n\n### User Action Required\n\nUsers should upgrade their `@link-assistant/agent` package:\n\n```bash\nbun install -g @link-assistant/agent@latest\n```\n\n### Related\n\n- Issue #1151\n- Agent PR #126: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/pull/126\n\nFixes #1151",
        "is_error": false
      }

Command line arguments\n    53→ * @param {boolean} compactJson - Whether to use compact JSON output\n    54→ * @returns {Promise<{sessionID: string, wasResumed: boolean, wasForked: boolean} | null>} - Session info or null if new session should be created\n    55→ * @export\n    56→ */\n    57→export async function resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson) {\n    58→  const resumeSessionID = argv.resume;\n    59→  const shouldContinue = argv.continue === true;\n    60→  const noFork = argv['no-fork'] === true;\n    61→\n    62→  // If neither --resume nor --continue is specified, return null to create new session\n    63→  if (!resumeSessionID && !shouldContinue) {\n    64→    return null;\n    65→  }\n    66→\n    67→  let targetSessionID = resumeSessionID;\n    68→\n    69→  // If --continue is specified, find the most recent session\n    70→  if (shouldContinue && !targetSessionID) {\n    71→    let mostRecentSession = null;\n    72→    let mostRecentTime = 0;\n    73→\n    74→    for await (const session of Session.list()) {\n    75→      // Skip child sessions (those with parentID) - find top-level sessions only\n    76→      if (session.parentID) {\n    77→        continue;\n    78→      }\n    79→\n    80→      if (session.time.updated > mostRecentTime) {\n    81→        mostRecentTime = session.time.updated;\n    82→        mostRecentSession = session;\n    83→      }\n    84→    }\n    85→\n    86→    if (!mostRecentSession) {\n    87→      outputStatus(\n    88→        {\n    89→          type: 'error',\n    90→          errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n    91→          message:\n    92→            'No existing sessions found to continue. Start a new session first.',\n    93→        },\n    94→        compactJson\n    95→      );\n    96→      process.exit(1);\n    97→    }\n    98→\n    99→    targetSessionID = mostRecentSession.id;\n   100→    log.info(() => ({\n   101→      message: 'Found most recent session to continue',\n   102→      sessionID: targetSessionID,\n   103→      title: mostRecentSession.title,\n   104→    }));\n   105→  }\n   106→\n   107→  // Verify the session exists\n   108→  let existingSession;\n   109→  try {\n   110→    existingSession = await Session.get(targetSessionID);\n   111→  } catch (_error) {\n   112→    // Session.get throws an error when the session doesn't exist\n   113→    outputStatus(\n   114→      {\n   115→        type: 'error',\n   116→        errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n   117→ JSON.stringify(status)\n    : JSON.stringify(status, null, 2);\n  console.error(json);\n}\n\n// Logger for resume operations\nconst log = Log.create({ service: 'resume' });\n\n/**\n * Resolve the session to use based on --resume, --continue, and --no-fork options.\n * Returns the session ID to use, handling forking as needed.\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @param {boolean} compactJson - Whether to use compact JSON output\n * @returns {Promise<{sessionID: string, wasResumed: boolean, wasForked: boolean} | null>} - Session info or null if new session should be created\n * @export\n */\nexport async function resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson) {\n  const resumeSessionID = argv.resume;\n  const shouldContinue = argv.continue === true;\n  const noFork = argv['no-fork'] === true;\n\n  // If neither --resume nor --continue is specified, return null to create new session\n  if (!resumeSessionID && !shouldContinue) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  let targetSessionID = resumeSessionID;\n\n  // If --continue is specified, find the most recent session\n  if (shouldContinue && !targetSessionID) {\n    let mostRecentSession = null;\n    let mostRecentTime = 0;\n\n    for await (const session of Session.list()) {\n      // Skip child sessions (those with parentID) - find top-level sessions only\n      if (session.parentID) {\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      if (session.time.updated > mostRecentTime) {\n        mostRecentTime = session.time.updated;\n        mostRecentSession = session;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (!mostRecentSession) {\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n          message:\n            'No existing sessions found to continue. Start a new session first.',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    targetSessionID = mostRecentSession.id;\n    log.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Found most recent session to continue',\n      sessionID: targetSessionID,\n      title: mostRecentSession.title,\n    }));\n  }\n\n  // Verify the session exists\n  let existingSession;\n  try {\n    existingSession = await Session.get(targetSessionID);\n  } catch (_error) {\n    // Session.get throws an error when the session doesn't exist\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n        message: `Session not found: ${targetSessionID}`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  if (!existingSession) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n        message: `Session not found: ${targetSessionID}`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  log.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Resuming session',\n    sessionID: targetSessionID,\n    title: existingSession.title,\n    noFork,\n  }));\n\n  // If --no-fork is specified, continue in the same session\n  if (noFork) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'status',\n        mode: 'resume',\n        message: `Continuing session without forking: ${targetSessionID}`,\n        sessionID: targetSessionID,\n        title: existingSession.title,\n        forked: false,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n\n    return {\n      sessionID: targetSessionID,\n      wasResumed: true,\n      wasForked: false,\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Fork the session to a new UUID (default behavior)\n  const forkedSession = await Session.fork({\n    sessionID: targetSessionID,\n  });\n\n  outputStatus(\n    {\n      type: 'status',\n      mode: 'resume',\n      message: `Forked session ${targetSessionID} to new session: ${forkedSession.id}`,\n      originalSessionID: targetSessionID,\n      sessionID: forkedSession.id,\n      title: forkedSession.title,\n      forked: true,\n    },\n    compactJson\n  );\n\n  log.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Forked session',\n    originalSessionID: targetSessionID,\n    newSessionID: forkedSession.id,\n  }));\n\n  return {\n    sessionID: forkedSession.id,\n    wasResumed: true,\n    wasForked: true,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Run server mode with continuous stdin input\n * Keeps the session alive and processes messages as they arrive\n */\nexport async function runContinuousServerMode(\n  argv,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n  const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n  let stdinReader = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Track if we're currently processing a message\n    let isProcessing = false;\n    const pendingMessages = [];\n\n    // Process messages from the queue\n    const processMessage = async (message) => {\n      if (isProcessing) {\n        pendingMessages.push(message);\n        return;\n      }\n\n      isProcessing = true;\n      const messageText = message.message || 'hi';\n      const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: messageText }];\n\n      // Create a promise to wait for this message to complete\n      const messagePromise = new Promise((resolve) => {\n        const checkIdle = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n          if (\n            event.type === 'session.idle' &&\n            event.properties.sessionID === sessionID\n          ) {\n            checkIdle();\n            resolve();\n          }\n        });\n      });\n\n      // Send message\n      fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            parts,\n            model: { providerID, modelID },\n            system: systemMessage,\n            appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n          }),\n        }\n      ).catch((error) => {\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n        });\n      });\n\n      await messagePromise;\n      isProcessing = false;\n\n      // Process next pending message if any\n      if (pendingMessages.length > 0) {\n        const nextMessage = pendingMessages.shift();\n        processMessage(nextMessage);\n      }\n    };\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    unsub = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n      if (event.type === 'message.part.updated') {\n        const part = event.properties.part;\n        if (part.sessionID !== sessionID) {\n          return;\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-start') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_start',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-finish') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_finish',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'text' && part.time?.end) {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'text',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'tool' && part.state.status === 'completed') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'tool_use',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (event.type === 'session.error') {\n        const props = event.properties;\n        if (props.sessionID !== sessionID || !props.error) {\n          return;\n        }\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: props.error,\n        });\n      }\n    });\n\n    // Create continuous stdin reader\n    stdinReader = createContinuousStdinReader({\n      interactive: isInteractive,\n      autoMerge,\n      onMessage: (message) => {\n        processMessage(message);\n      },\n    });\n\n    // Wait for stdin to end (EOF or close)\n    await new Promise((resolve) => {\n      const checkRunning = setInterval(() => {\n        if (!stdinReader.isRunning()) {\n          clearInterval(checkRunning);\n          // Wait for any pending messages to complete\n          const waitForPending = () => {\n            if (!isProcessing && pendingMessages.length === 0) {\n              resolve();\n            } else {\n              setTimeout(waitForPending, 100);\n            }\n          };\n          waitForPending();\n        }\n      }, 100);\n\n      // Also handle SIGINT\n      process.on('SIGINT', () => {\n        outputStatus(\n          {\n            type: 'status',\n            message: 'Received SIGINT. Shutting down...',\n          },\n          compactJson\n        );\n        clearInterval(checkRunning);\n        resolve();\n      });\n    });\n  } finally {\n    if (stdinReader) {\n      stdinReader.stop();\n    }\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Run direct mode with continuous stdin input\n * Keeps the session alive and processes messages as they arrive\n */\nexport async function runContinuousDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n  const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n\n  let unsub = null;\n  let stdinReader = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Track if we're currently processing a message\n    let isProcessing = false;\n    const pendingMessages = [];\n\n    // Process messages from the queue\n    const processMessage = async (message) => {\n      if (isProcessing) {\n        pendingMessages.push(message);\n        return;\n      }\n\n      isProcessing = true;\n      const messageText = message.message || 'hi';\n      const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: messageText }];\n\n      // Create a promise to wait for this message to complete\n      const messagePromise = new Promise((resolve) => {\n        const checkIdle = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n          if (\n            event.type === 'session.idle' &&\n            event.properties.sessionID === sessionID\n          ) {\n            checkIdle();\n            resolve();\n          }\n        });\n      });\n\n      // Send message directly\n      SessionPrompt.prompt({\n        sessionID,\n        parts,\n        model: { providerID, modelID },\n        system: systemMessage,\n        appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n      }).catch((error) => {\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n        });\n      });\n\n      await messagePromise;\n      isProcessing = false;\n\n      // Process next pending message if any\n      if (pendingMessages.length > 0) {\n        const nextMessage = pendingMessages.shift();\n        processMessage(nextMessage);\n      }\n    };\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    unsub = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n      if (event.type === 'message.part.updated') {\n        const part = event.properties.part;\n        if (part.sessionID !== sessionID) {\n          return;\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-start') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_start',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'tool' && part.state.status === 'completed') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'tool_use',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (event.type === 'session.error') {\n        const props = event.properties;\n        if (props.sessionID !== sessionID || !props.error) {\n          return;\n        }\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: props.error,\n        });\n      }\n    });\n\n    // Create continuous stdin reader\n    stdinReader = createContinuousStdinReader({\n      interactive: isInteractive,\n      autoMerge,\n      onMessage: (message) => {\n        processMessage(message);\n      },\n    });\n\n    // Wait for stdin to end (EOF or close)\n    await new Promise((resolve) => {\n      const checkRunning = setInterval(() => {\n        if (!stdinReader.isRunning()) {\n          clearInterval(checkRunning);\n          // Wait for any pending messages to complete\n          const waitForPending = () => {\n            if (!isProcessing && pendingMessages.length === 0) {\n              resolve();\n            } else {\n              setTimeout(waitForPending, 100);\n            }\n          };\n          waitForPending();\n        }\n      }, 100);\n\n      // Also handle SIGINT\n      process.on('SIGINT', () => {\n        outputStatus(\n          {\n            type: 'status',\n            message: Shutting down...',\n          },\n          compactJson\n        );\n        clearInterval(checkRunning);\n        resolve();\n      });\n    });\n  } finally {\n    if (stdinReader) {\n      stdinReader.stop();\n    }\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n","numLines":621,"startLine":1,"totalLines":621}}}

'../package.json');\n  const pkgContent = readFileSync(pkgPath, 'utf8');\n  pkg = JSON.parse(pkgContent);\n}\n\n// Track if any errors occurred during execution\nlet hasError = false;\n\n// Install global error handlers to ensure non-zero exit codes\nprocess.on('uncaughtException', (error) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  console.error(\n    JSON.stringify(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        errorType: error.name || 'UncaughtException',\n        message: error.message,\n        stack: error.stack,\n      },\n      null,\n      2\n    )\n  );\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\nprocess.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, _promise) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  console.error(\n    JSON.stringify(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n        message: reason?.message || String(reason),\n        stack: reason?.stack,\n      },\n      null,\n      2\n    )\n  );\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\n/**\n * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Output JSON status message to stderr\n * This prevents the status message from interfering with JSON output parsing\n * @param {object} status - Status object to output\n * @param {boolean} compact - If true, output compact JSON (single line)\n */\nfunction outputStatus(status, compact = false) {\n  const json = compact\n    ? JSON.stringify(status)\n    : JSON.stringify(status, null, 2);\n  console.error(json);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse model configuration from argv\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { providerID, modelID }\n */\nasync function parseModelConfig(argv) {\n  // Parse model argument (handle model IDs with slashes like groq/qwen/qwen3-32b)\n  const modelParts = argv.model.split('/');\n  let providerID = modelParts[0] || 'opencode';\n  let modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/') || 'grok-code';\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('./auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified a model, use it with claude-oauth provider\n    // If not, use claude-oauth/claude-sonnet-4-5 as default\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' && modelID === 'grok-code') {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using OAuth credentials anyway.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID };\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runServerMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session directly\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt directly without HTTP\n    SessionPrompt.prompt({\n      sessionID,\n      parts,\n      model: {\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n      },\n      system: systemMessage,\n      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n    }).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n    // Parse command line arguments with subcommands\n    const yargsInstance = yargs(hideBin(process.argv))\n      .scriptName('agent')\n      .usage('$0 [command] [options]')\n      .version(pkg.version)\n      // MCP subcommand\n      .command(McpCommand)\n      // Auth subcommand\n      .command(AuthCommand)\n      // Default command for agent mode (when no subcommand specified)\n      .command({\n        command: '$0',\n        describe: 'Run agent in interactive or stdin mode (default)',\n        builder: (yargs) =>\n          yargs\n            .option('model', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Model to use in format providerID/modelID',\n              default: 'opencode/grok-code',\n            })\n            .option('json-standard', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'JSON output format standard: \"opencode\" (default) or \"claude\" (experimental)',\n              default: 'opencode',\n              choices: ['opencode', 'claude'],\n            })\n            .option('system-message', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Full override of the system message',\n            })\n            .option('system-message-file', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Full override of the system message from file',\n            })\n            .option('append-system-message', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Append to the default system message',\n            })\n            .option('append-system-message-file', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Append to the default system message from file',\n            })\n            .option('server', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description: 'Run in server mode (default)',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('verbose', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable verbose mode to debug API requests (shows system prompt, token counts, etc.)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('dry-run', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Simulate operations without making actual API calls or package installations (useful for testing)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('use-existing-claude-oauth', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Use existing Claude OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (from Claude Code CLI)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('prompt', {\n              alias: 'p',\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'Prompt message to send directly (bypasses stdin reading)',\n            })\n            .option('disable-stdin', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Disable stdin streaming mode (requires --prompt or shows help)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('stdin-stream-timeout', {\n              type: 'number',\n              description:\n                'Optional timeout in milliseconds for stdin reading (default: no timeout)',\n            })\n            .option('auto-merge-queued-messages', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable auto-merging of rapidly arriving input lines into single messages (default: true)',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('interactive', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable interactive mode to accept manual input as plain text strings (default: true). Use --no-interactive to only accept JSON input.',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('always-accept-stdin', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Keep accepting stdin input even after the agent finishes work (default: true). Use --no-always-accept-stdin for single-message mode.',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('compact-json', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Output compact JSON (single line) instead of pretty-printed JSON (default: false). Useful for program-to-program communication.',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('resume', {\n              alias: 'r',\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'Resume a specific session by ID. By default, forks the session with a new UUID. Use --no-fork to continue in the same session.',\n            })\n            .option('continue', {\n              alias: 'c',\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Continue the most recent session. By default, forks the session with a new UUID. Use --no-fork to continue in the same session.',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('no-fork', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'When used with --resume or --continue, continue in the same session without forking to a new UUID.',\n              default: false,\n            }),\n        handler: async (argv) => {\n          const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n          // Check if --prompt flag was provided\n          if (argv.prompt) {\n            // Direct prompt mode - bypass stdin entirely\n            const request = { message: argv.prompt };\n            await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // Check if --disable-stdin was set without --prompt\n          if (argv['disable-stdin']) {\n            // Output a helpful message suggesting to use --prompt\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'error',\n                message:\n                  'No prompt provided. Use -p/--prompt to specify a message, or remove --disable-stdin to read from stdin.',\n                hint: 'Example: agent -p \"Hello, how are you?\"',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n\n          // Check if stdin is a TTY (interactive terminal)\n          if (process.stdin.isTTY) {\n            // Enter interactive terminal mode with continuous listening\n            const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n            const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n            const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n            // Exit if --no-always-accept-stdin is set (single message mode not supported in TTY)\n            if (!alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n              outputStatus(\n                {\n                  type: 'error',\n                  message:\n                    'Single message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin) is not supported in interactive terminal mode.',\n                  hint: 'Use piped input or --prompt for single messages.',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                mode: 'interactive-terminal',\n                message:\n                  'Agent CLI in interactive terminal mode. Type your message and press Enter.',\n                hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n                acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                  ? ['JSON object with \"message\" field', 'Plain text']\n                  : ['JSON object with \"message\" field'],\n                options: {\n                  interactive: isInteractive,\n                  autoMergeQueuedMessages: autoMerge,\n                  alwaysAcceptStdin,\n                  compactJson,\n                },\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n\n            // Use continuous mode for interactive terminal\n            await runContinuousAgentMode(argv);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // stdin is piped - enter stdin listening mode\n          const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n          const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n          const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n          outputStatus(\n            {\n              type: 'status',\n              mode: 'stdin-stream',\n              message: alwaysAcceptStdin\n                ? 'Agent CLI in continuous listening mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.'\n                : 'Agent CLI in single-message mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.',\n              hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n              acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                ? ['JSON object with \"message\" field', 'Plain text']\n                : ['JSON object with \"message\" field'],\n              options: {\n                interactive: isInteractive,\n                autoMergeQueuedMessages: autoMerge,\n                alwaysAcceptStdin,\n                compactJson,\n              },\n            },\n            compactJson\n          );\n\n          // Use continuous mode if --always-accept-stdin is enabled (default)\n          if (alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n            await runContinuousAgentMode(argv);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // Single-message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin)\n          const timeout = argv['stdin-stream-timeout'] ?? null;\n          const input = await readStdinWithTimeout(timeout);\n          const trimmedInput = input.trim();\n\n          if (trimmedInput === '') {\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                message: 'No input received. Exiting.',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            yargsInstance.showHelp();\n            process.exit(0);\n          }\n\n          // Try to parse as JSON, if it fails treat it as plain text message\n          let request;\n          try {\n            request = JSON.parse(trimmedInput);\n          } catch (_e) {\n            // Not JSON\n            if (!isInteractive) {\n              // In non-interactive mode, only accept JSON\n              outputStatus(\n                {\n                  type: 'error',\n                  message:\n                    'Invalid JSON input. In non-interactive mode (--no-interactive), only JSON input is accepted.',\n                  hint: 'Use --interactive to accept plain text, or provide valid JSON: {\"message\": \"your text\"}',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n            // In interactive mode, treat as plain text message\n            request = {\n              message: trimmedInput,\n            };\n          }\n\n          // Run agent mode\n          await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n        },\n      })\n      // Initialize logging early for all CLI commands\n      // This prevents debug output from appearing in CLI unless --verbose is used\n      .middleware(async (argv) => {\n        // Set verbose flag if requested\n        if (argv.verbose) {\n          Flag.setVerbose(true);\n        }\n\n        // Set dry-run flag if requested\n        if (argv['dry-run']) {\n          Flag.setDryRun(true);\n        }\n\n        // Initialize logging system\n        // - If verbose: print logs to stderr for debugging in JSON format\n        // - Otherwise: write logs to file to keep CLI output clean\n        await Log.init({\n          print: Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n          level: Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE ? 'DEBUG' : 'INFO',\n        });\n      })\n      .fail((msg, err, yargs) => {\n        // Handle errors from command handlers\n        if (err) {\n          // Check if it's a CancelledError (user pressed ESC)\n          if (UI.CancelledError.isInstance(err)) {\n            // Exit silently without showing error or help text\n            process.exit(0);\n          }\n\n          // Format other errors using FormatError\n          const formatted = FormatError(err);\n          if (formatted) {\n            console.error(formatted);\n          } else {\n            // Fallback to default error formatting\n            console.error(err.message || err);\n          }\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n\n        // Handle validation errors (msg without err)\n        if (msg) {\n          console.error(msg);\n          console.error(`\\n${yargs.help()}`);\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n      })\n      .help();\n\n    // Parse arguments (handlers will be called automatically)\n    await yargsInstance.argv;\n  } catch (error) {\n    hasError = true;\n    console.error(\n      JSON.stringify(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          errorType: error instanceof Error ? error.name : 'Error',\n          message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n          stack: error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined,\n        },\n        null,\n        2\n      )\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nmain();\n","numLines":916,"startLine":1,"totalLines":916}}}

[2026-01-23T19:32:33.819Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "model": "claude-opus-4-5-20251101",
    "id": "msg_012BtrGuYfBRKBfwthV3jgPQ",
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_01BHMFMNg5x3XwdXzEjq2gRL",
        "name": "Write",
        "input": {
          "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1769196616847/docs/case-studies/issue-131/README.md",
          "content": "# Case Study: Agent CLI outputs stderr instead of stdout (Issue #131)\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThe Agent CLI incorrectly sends all output to stderr instead of stdout, making it difficult for parent processes to properly separate normal output from error messages. This case study documents the root cause analysis, findings, and the implemented solution.\n\n## Issue Details\n\n- **Issue Number**: #131\n- **Repository**: link-assistant/agent\n- **Reporter**: konard\n- **Date Reported**: January 2026\n- **Related Issue**: [hive-mind#1151](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1151)\n- **Related PR**: [hive-mind#1163](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1163)\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nThe Agent CLI sends ALL output to stderr, including:\n- Status messages (mode, startup info, options)\n- Verbose log messages (`{\"log\":{\"level\":\"info\",...}}`)\n- Structured events (`{\"type\":\"step_start\",...}`, `{\"type\":\"tool_use\",...}`, etc.)\n\nThis behavior makes it impossible for consuming applications to:\n1. Properly parse stdout for structured JSON output\n2. Separate normal operational output from actual errors\n3. Use standard Unix pipeline patterns\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary Locations Using stderr\n\nAfter thorough code analysis, the following locations were identified as sending non-error output to stderr:\n\n1. **`js/src/index.js:143-148`**: `outputStatus()` function uses `console.error(json)`\n   - Outputs status messages to stderr instead of stdout\n\n2. **`js/src/cli/continuous-mode.js:39-44`**: Duplicate `outputStatus()` function\n   - Same issue as above\n\n3. **`js/src/util/log.ts:93`**: Default log writer uses `Bun.stderr.write(msg)`\n   - All log messages default to stderr\n\n4. **`js/src/util/log-lazy.ts:99-100`**: Lazy logging uses `process.stderr.write(json + '\\n')`\n   - Lazy log evaluation writes to stderr\n\n5. **`js/src/cli/ui.ts:26-50`**: UI output functions use `process.stderr.write()`\n   - Progress and status UI elements go to stderr\n\n### Why This Happened\n\nThe original design decision appears to have been:\n- Keep stdout \"clean\" for structured JSON parsing\n- Use stderr for \"meta\" information like logs and status\n\nHowever, this violates the Unix convention where:\n- stdout = normal program output (structured data in this case)\n- stderr = error messages and diagnostics only\n\n## Impact Assessment\n\n### Affected Use Cases\n\n1. **Tool Integration**: The `--tool agent` mode in hive-mind fails because:\n   - hive-mind's `agent.lib.mjs` expected JSON on stdout\n   - All JSON was actually coming from stderr\n   - stderr was not being parsed as JSON\n\n2. **Pipeline Usage**: Standard Unix pipelines don't work:\n   ```bash\n   # This captures nothing because output is on stderr\n   agent -p \"hello\" | jq .\n\n   # This works but conflates errors with normal output\n   agent -p \"hello\" 2>&1 | jq .\n   ```\n\n3. **Parent Process Integration**: Any process spawning the agent must:\n   - Read from stderr for normal output\n   - Cannot distinguish actual errors from normal output\n\n## Solution Design\n\n### Requirements (from issue description)\n\n1. Output everything except errors to stdout by default\n2. All JSON output must have a `type` field\n3. Flatten log statements from `{ \"log\": { \"level\": \"info\", ... } }` to `{ \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"info\", ... }`\n4. Log formatting should be configurable (consistent with other JSON output)\n5. User input confirmation should be echoed in JSON format\n\n### Implementation Changes\n\n#### 1. Status Output to stdout\n\nModified `outputStatus()` in both `index.js` and `continuous-mode.js` to:\n- Use `process.stdout.write()` for non-error status messages\n- Continue using `console.error()` only for actual error types\n\n#### 2. Log Output Configuration\n\nModified `Log` module in `util/log.ts` to:\n- Default to stdout for log output\n- Allow configuration for stderr when specifically needed\n- Support the new flattened format with `type: \"log\"`\n\n#### 3. Flattened Log Format\n\nChanged from:\n```json\n{\"log\":{\"level\":\"info\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-01-23T...\",\"message\":\"...\"}}\n```\n\nTo:\n```json\n{\"type\":\"log\",\"level\":\"info\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-01-23T...\",\"message\":\"...\"}\n```\n\n#### 4. Consistent JSON Formatting\n\nAll JSON output now:\n- Respects `--compact-json` flag\n- Has consistent `type` field\n- Uses same formatting function\n\n## Files Changed\n\n1. `js/src/index.js` - Output routing\n2. `js/src/cli/continuous-mode.js` - Continuous mode output\n3. `js/src/util/log.ts` - Log formatting and output destination\n4. `js/src/json-standard/index.ts` - JSON event types\n5. `js/src/cli/ui.ts` - UI output (kept on stderr as appropriate for interactive UI)\n\n## Testing\n\n### Manual Testing\n\n```bash\n# Verify status output goes to stdout\nagent -p \"hello\" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | jq .type\n\n# Verify errors still go to stderr\nagent --invalid-option 2>&1 1>/dev/null | head -1\n\n# Verify log output format\nagent --verbose -p \"hello\" | grep '\"type\":\"log\"'\n```\n\n### Automated Tests\n\nAdded tests in `js/tests/` to verify:\n- Status messages go to stdout\n- Error messages go to stderr\n- Log format is correct\n- `type` field is present in all JSON output\n\n## Lessons Learned\n\n1. **Follow Unix Conventions**: stdout for data, stderr for errors\n2. **Consistent Output Format**: All JSON should have a `type` field for easy parsing\n3. **Document Output Contract**: API consumers need clear documentation on output format\n4. **Test Output Streams**: Automated tests should verify which stream receives which output\n\n## References\n\n- [Issue #131](https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/131)\n- [PR #132](https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/pull/132)\n- [hive-mind PR #1163](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1163)\n- [Unix Philosophy - Standard Streams](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_streams)\n"
        }
      }
    ],
    "stop_reason": null,
    "stop_sequence": null,
    "usage": {
      "input_tokens": 0,
      "cache_creation_input_tokens": 109,
      "cache_read_input_tokens": 56338,
      "cache_creation": {
        "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 109,
        "ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 0
      },
      "output_tokens": 1,
      "service_tier": "standard"
    },
    "context_management": null
  },
  "parent_tool_use_id": null,
  "session_id": "3ac5805a-e7bf-44c8-bdce-a4ff0de215db",
  "uuid": "76d7789a-5c03-4318-94e8-6ab18653cd3c"

JSON.stringify(message)\n    : JSON.stringify(message, null, 2);\n}\n\n/**\n * Write a message to stdout (for normal output)\n * @param message - The message object to output\n * @param compact - Override the global compact setting\n */\nexport function writeStdout(\n  message: OutputMessage,\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const json = formatJson(message, compact);\n  process.stdout.write(json + EOL);\n}\n\n/**\n * Write a message to stderr (for errors only)\n * @param message - The message object to output\n * @param compact - Override the global compact setting\n */\nexport function writeStderr(\n  message: OutputMessage,\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const json = formatJson(message, compact);\n  process.stderr.write(json + EOL);\n}\n\n/**\n * Output a message to the appropriate stream based on type\n * - Errors go to stderr\n * - Everything else goes to stdout\n *\n * @param message - The message object to output\n * @param compact - Override the global compact setting\n */\nexport function output(message: OutputMessage, compact?: boolean): void {\n  if (message.type === 'error') {\n    writeStderr(message, compact);\n  } else {\n    writeStdout(message, compact);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Output a status message to stdout\n */\nexport function outputStatus(\n  status: Omit<OutputMessage, 'type'> & { type?: 'status' | 'error' | 'warning' },\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const message: OutputMessage = {\n    type: 'status',\n    ...status,\n  };\n  output(message, compact);\n}\n\n/**\n * Output an error message to stderr\n */\nexport function outputError(\n  error: {\n    errorType?: string;\n    message: string;\n    hint?: string;\n    stack?: string;\n    [key: string]: unknown;\n  },\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const message: OutputMessage = {\n    type: 'error',\n    ...error,\n  };\n  writeStderr(message, compact);\n}\n\n/**\n * Output a warning message to stdout\n */\nexport function outputWarning(\n };\n  writeStdout(message, compact);\n}\n\n/**\n * Output a log message to stdout\n * This uses the flattened format: { \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"info\", ... }\n */\nexport function outputLog(\n  log: {\n    level: 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error';\n    message?: string;\n    timestamp?: string;\n    [key: string]: unknown;\n  },\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const message: string;\n    parsed?: unknown;\n    format?: 'json' | 'text';\n    [key: string]: unknown;\n  },\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const message: OutputMessage = {\n    type: 'input',\n    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    ...input,\n  };\n  writeStdout(message, compact);\n}\n\n// Re-export for backward compatibility\nexport { output as write };\n"
