'./cli/cmd/mcp.ts';\nimport { AuthCommand } from './cli/cmd/auth.ts';\nimport { Flag } from './flag/flag.ts';\nimport { FormatError } from './cli/error.ts';\nimport { UI } from './cli/ui.ts';\nimport {\n  runContinuousServerMode,\n  runContinuousDirectMode,\n  resolveResumeSession,\n} from './cli/continuous-mode.js';\nimport { createBusEventSubscription } from './cli/event-handler.js';\nimport {\n  outputStatus,\n  outputError,\n  setCompactJson,\n} from './cli/output.ts';\nimport { createRequire } from 'module';\nimport { readFileSync } from 'fs';\nimport { dirname, join } from 'path';\nimport { fileURLToPath } from 'url';\n\nconst require = createRequire(import.meta.url);\nlet pkg;\ntry {\n  pkg = require('../package.json');\n} catch (_e) {\n  // Fallback: read package.json directly\n  const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\n  const pkgPath = join(__dirname, '../package.json');\n  const pkgContent = readFileSync(pkgPath, 'utf8');\n  pkg = JSON.parse(pkgContent);\n}\n\n// Track if any errors occurred during execution\nlet hasError = false;\n\n// Install global error handlers to ensure non-zero exit codes\nprocess.on('uncaughtException', (error) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: error.name || 'UncaughtException',\n    message: error.message,\n    stack: error.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\nprocess.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, _promise) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n    message: reason?.message || String(reason),\n    stack: reason?.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\n/**\n * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n// outputStatus is now imported from './cli/output.ts'\n// It outputs to stdout for non-error messages, stderr for errors\n\n/**\n * Parse model configuration from argv\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { providerID, modelID }\n */\nasync function parseModelConfig(argv) {\n  // Parse model argument (handle model IDs with slashes like groq/qwen/qwen3-32b)\n  const modelParts = argv.model.split('/');\n  let providerID = modelParts[0] || 'opencode';\n  let modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/') || 'grok-code';\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('./auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified a model, use it with claude-oauth provider\n    // If not, use claude-oauth/claude-sonnet-4-5 as default\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' && modelID === 'grok-code') {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using OAuth credentials anyway.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID };\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runServerMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session directly\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt directly without HTTP\n    SessionPrompt.prompt({\n      sessionID,\n      parts,\n      model: {\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n      },\n      system: systemMessage,\n      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n    }).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n    // Parse command line arguments with subcommands\n    const yargsInstance = yargs(hideBin(process.argv))\n      .scriptName('agent')\n      .usage('$0 [command] [options]')\n      .version(pkg.version)\n      // MCP subcommand\n      .command(McpCommand)\n      // Auth subcommand\n      .command(AuthCommand)\n      // Default command for agent mode (when no subcommand specified)\n      .command({\n        command: '$0',\n        describe: 'Run agent in interactive or stdin mode (default)',\n        builder: (yargs) =>\n          yargs\n            .option('model', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Model to use in format providerID/modelID',\n              default: 'opencode/grok-code',\n            })\n            .option('json-standard', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'JSON output format standard: \"opencode\" (default) or \"claude\" (experimental)',\n              default: 'opencode',\n              choices: ['opencode', 'claude'],\n            })\n            .option('system-message', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Full override of the system message',\n            })\n            .option('system-message-file', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Full override of the system message from file',\n            })\n            .option('append-system-message', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Append to the default system message',\n            })\n            .option('append-system-message-file', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Append to the default system message from file',\n            })\n            .option('server', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description: 'Run in server mode (default)',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('verbose', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable verbose mode to debug API requests (shows system prompt, token counts, etc.)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('dry-run', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Simulate operations without making actual API calls or package installations (useful for testing)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('use-existing-claude-oauth', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Use existing Claude OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (from Claude Code CLI)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('prompt', {\n              alias: 'p',\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'Prompt message to send directly (bypasses stdin reading)',\n            })\n            .option('disable-stdin', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Disable stdin streaming mode (requires --prompt or shows help)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('stdin-stream-timeout', {\n              type: 'number',\n              description:\n                'Optional timeout in milliseconds for stdin reading (default: no timeout)',\n            })\n            .option('auto-merge-queued-messages', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable auto-merging of rapidly arriving input lines into single messages (default: true)',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('interactive', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable interactive mode to accept manual input as plain text strings (default: true). Use --no-interactive to only accept JSON input.',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('always-accept-stdin', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Keep accepting stdin input even after the agent finishes work (default: true). Use --no-always-accept-stdin for single-message mode.',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('compact-json', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Output compact JSON (single line) instead of pretty-printed JSON (default: false). Useful for program-to-program communication.',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('resume', {\n              alias: 'r',\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'Resume a specific session by ID. By default, forks the session with a new UUID. Use --no-fork to continue in the same session.',\n            })\n            .option('continue', {\n              alias: 'c',\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Continue the most recent session. By default, forks the session with a new UUID. Use --no-fork to continue in the same session.',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('no-fork', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'When used with --resume or --continue, continue in the same session without forking to a new UUID.',\n              default: false,\n            }),\n        handler: async (argv) => {\n          const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n          // Check if --prompt flag was provided\n          if (argv.prompt) {\n            // Direct prompt mode - bypass stdin entirely\n            const request = { message: argv.prompt };\n            await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // Check if --disable-stdin was set without --prompt\n          if (argv['disable-stdin']) {\n            // Output a helpful message suggesting to use --prompt\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'error',\n                message:\n                  'No prompt provided. Use -p/--prompt to specify a message, or remove --disable-stdin to read from stdin.',\n                hint: 'Example: agent -p \"Hello, how are you?\"',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n\n          // Check if stdin is a TTY (interactive terminal)\n          if (process.stdin.isTTY) {\n            // Enter interactive terminal mode with continuous listening\n            const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n            const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n            const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n            // Exit if --no-always-accept-stdin is set (single message mode not supported in TTY)\n            if (!alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n              outputStatus(\n                {\n                  type: 'error',\n                  message:\n                    'Single message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin) is not supported in interactive terminal mode.',\n                  hint: 'Use piped input or --prompt for single messages.',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                mode: 'interactive-terminal',\n                message:\n                  'Agent CLI in interactive terminal mode. Type your message and press Enter.',\n                hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n                acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                  ? ['JSON object with \"message\" field', 'Plain text']\n                  : ['JSON object with \"message\" field'],\n                options: {\n                  interactive: isInteractive,\n                  autoMergeQueuedMessages: autoMerge,\n                  alwaysAcceptStdin,\n                  compactJson,\n                },\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n\n            // Use continuous mode for interactive terminal\n            await runContinuousAgentMode(argv);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // stdin is piped - enter stdin listening mode\n          const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n          const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n          const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n          outputStatus(\n            {\n              type: 'status',\n              mode: 'stdin-stream',\n              message: alwaysAcceptStdin\n                ? 'Agent CLI in continuous listening mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.'\n                : 'Agent CLI in single-message mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.',\n              hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n              acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                ? ['JSON object with \"message\" field', 'Plain text']\n                : ['JSON object with \"message\" field'],\n              options: {\n                interactive: isInteractive,\n                autoMergeQueuedMessages: autoMerge,\n                alwaysAcceptStdin,\n                compactJson,\n              },\n            },\n            compactJson\n          );\n\n          // Use continuous mode if --always-accept-stdin is enabled (default)\n          if (alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n            await runContinuousAgentMode(argv);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // Single-message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin)\n          const timeout = argv['stdin-stream-timeout'] ?? null;\n          const input = await readStdinWithTimeout(timeout);\n          const trimmedInput = input.trim();\n\n          if (trimmedInput === '') {\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                message: 'No input received. Exiting.',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            yargsInstance.showHelp();\n            process.exit(0);\n          }\n\n          // Try to parse as JSON, if it fails treat it as plain text message\n          let request;\n          try {\n            request = JSON.parse(trimmedInput);\n          } catch (_e) {\n            // Not JSON\n            if (!isInteractive) {\n              // In non-interactive mode, only accept JSON\n              outputStatus(\n                {\n                  type: 'error',\n                  message:\n                    'Invalid JSON input. In non-interactive mode (--no-interactive), only JSON input is accepted.',\n                  hint: 'Use --interactive to accept plain text, or provide valid JSON: {\"message\": \"your text\"}',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n            // In interactive mode, treat as plain text message\n            request = {\n              message: trimmedInput,\n            };\n          }\n\n          // Run agent mode\n          await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n        },\n      })\n      // Initialize logging early for all CLI commands\n      // This prevents debug output from appearing in CLI unless --verbose is used\n      .middleware(async (argv) => {\n        // Set verbose flag if requested\n        if (argv.verbose) {\n          Flag.setVerbose(true);\n        }\n\n        // Set dry-run flag if requested\n        if (argv['dry-run']) {\n          Flag.setDryRun(true);\n        }\n\n        // Initialize logging system\n        // - If verbose: print logs to stderr for debugging in JSON format\n        // - Otherwise: write logs to file to keep CLI output clean\n        await Log.init({\n          print: Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n          level: Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE ? 'DEBUG' : 'INFO',\n        });\n      })\n      .fail((msg, err, yargs) => {\n        // Handle errors from command handlers\n        if (err) {\n          // Check if it's a CancelledError (user pressed ESC)\n          if (UI.CancelledError.isInstance(err)) {\n            // Exit silently without showing error or help text\n            process.exit(0);\n          }\n\n          // Format other errors using FormatError\n          const formatted = FormatError(err);\n          if (formatted) {\n            console.error(formatted);\n          } else {\n            // Fallback to default error formatting\n            console.error(err.message || err);\n          }\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n\n        // Handle validation errors (msg without err)\n        if (msg) {\n          console.error(msg);\n          console.error(`\\n${yargs.help()}`);\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n      })\n      .help();\n\n    // Parse arguments (handlers will be called automatically)\n    await yargsInstance.argv;\n  } catch (error) {\n    hasError = true;\n    console.error(\n      JSON.stringify(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          errorType: error instanceof Error ? error.name : 'Error',\n          message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n          stack: error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined,\n        },\n        null,\n        2\n      )\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nmain();\n",

require('../package.json');\n} catch (_e) {\n  // Fallback: read package.json directly\n  const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\n  const pkgPath = join(__dirname, '../package.json');\n  const pkgContent = readFileSync(pkgPath, 'utf8');\n  pkg = JSON.parse(pkgContent);\n}\n\n// Track if any errors occurred during execution\nlet hasError = false;\n\n// Install global error handlers to ensure non-zero exit codes\nprocess.on('uncaughtException', (error) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: error.name || 'UncaughtException',\n    message: error.message,\n    stack: error.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\nprocess.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, _promise) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n    message: reason?.message || String(reason),\n    stack: reason?.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\n/**\n * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n// outputStatus is now imported from './cli/output.ts'\n// It outputs to stdout for non-error messages, stderr for errors\n\n/**\n * Parse model configuration from argv\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { providerID, modelID }\n */\nasync function parseModelConfig(argv) {\n  // Parse model argument (handle model IDs with slashes like groq/qwen/qwen3-32b)\n  const modelParts = argv.model.split('/');\n  let providerID = modelParts[0] || 'opencode';\n  let modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/') || 'grok-code';\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('./auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified a model, use it with claude-oauth provider\n    // If not, use claude-oauth/claude-sonnet-4-5 as default\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' && modelID === 'grok-code') {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using OAuth credentials anyway.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID };\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runServerMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': [2026-01-23T19:33:53.839Z] [INFO] tException', (error) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: error.name || 'UncaughtException',\n    message: error.message,\n    stack: error.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\nprocess.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, _promise) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n    message: reason?.message || String(reason),\n    stack: reason?.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\n/**\n * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n// outputStatus is now imported from './cli/output.ts'\n// It outputs to stdout for non-error messages, stderr for errors\n\n/**\n * Parse model configuration from argv\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { providerID, modelID }\n */\nasync function parseModelConfig(argv) {\n  // Parse model argument (handle model IDs with slashes like groq/qwen/qwen3-32b)\n  const modelParts = argv.model.split('/');\n  let providerID = modelParts[0] || 'opencode';\n  let modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/') || 'grok-code';\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('./auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified a model, use it with claude-oauth provider\n    // If not, use claude-oauth/claude-sonnet-4-5 as default\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' && modelID === 'grok-code') {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using OAuth credentials anyway.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID };\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      console.error(\n        `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runServerMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session directly\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt directly without HTTP\n    SessionPrompt.prompt({\n      sessionID,\n      parts,\n      model: {\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n      },\n      system: systemMessage,\n      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n    }).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n    // Parse command line arguments with subcommands\n    const yargsInstance = yargs(hideBin(process.argv))\n      .scriptName('agent')\n      .usage('$0 [command] [options]')\n      .version(pkg.version)\n      // MCP subcommand\n      .command(McpCommand)\n      // Auth subcommand\n      .command(AuthCommand)\n      // Default command for agent mode (when no subcommand specified)\n      .command({\n        command: '$0',\n        describe: 'Run agent in interactive or stdin mode (default)',\n        builder: (yargs) =>\n          yargs\n            .option('model', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Model to use in format providerID/modelID',\n              default: 'opencode/grok-code',\n            })\n            .option('json-standard', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'JSON output format standard: \"opencode\" (default) or \"claude\" (experimental)',\n              default: 'opencode',\n              choices: ['opencode', 'claude'],\n            })\n            .option('system-message', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Full override of the system message',\n            })\n            .option('system-message-file', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Full override of the system message from file',\n            })\n            .option('append-system-message', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Append to the default system message',\n            })\n            .option('append-system-message-file', {\n              type: 'string',\n              description: 'Append to the default system message from file',\n            })\n            .option('server', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description: 'Run in server mode (default)',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('verbose', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable verbose mode to debug API requests (shows system prompt, token counts, etc.)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('dry-run', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Simulate operations without making actual API calls or package installations (useful for testing)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('use-existing-claude-oauth', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Use existing Claude OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (from Claude Code CLI)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('prompt', {\n              alias: 'p',\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'Prompt message to send directly (bypasses stdin reading)',\n            })\n            .option('disable-stdin', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Disable stdin streaming mode (requires --prompt or shows help)',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('stdin-stream-timeout', {\n              type: 'number',\n              description:\n                'Optional timeout in milliseconds for stdin reading (default: no timeout)',\n            })\n            .option('auto-merge-queued-messages', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Enable auto-merging of rapidly arriving input lines into single interactive mode to accept manual input as plain text strings (default: true). Use --no-interactive to only accept JSON input.',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('always-accept-stdin', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Keep accepting stdin input even after the agent finishes work (default: true). Use --no-always-accept-stdin for single-message mode.',\n              default: true,\n            })\n            .option('compact-json', {\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Output compact JSON (single line) instead of pretty-printed JSON (default: false). Useful for program-to-program communication.',\n              default: false,\n            })\n            .option('resume', {\n              alias: 'r',\n              type: 'string',\n              description:\n                'Resume a specific session by ID. By default, forks the session with a new UUID. Use --no-fork to continue in the same session.',\n            })\n            .option('continue', {\n              alias: 'c',\n              type: 'boolean',\n              description:\n                'Continue the most recent session. Use -p/--prompt to specify a message, or remove --disable-stdin to read from stdin.',\n                hint: 'Example: agent -p \"Hello, how are you?\"',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n\n          // Check if stdin is a TTY (interactive terminal)\n          if (process.stdin.isTTY) {\n            // Enter interactive terminal mode with continuous listening\n            const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n            const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n            const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n            // Exit if --no-always-accept-stdin is set (single message mode not supported in TTY)\n            if (!alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n              outputStatus(\n                {\n                  type: 'error',\n                  message:\n                    'Single message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin) is not supported in interactive terminal mode.',\n                  hint: 'Use piped input or --prompt for single messages.',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                mode: 'interactive-terminal',\n                message:\n                  'Agent CLI in interactive terminal mode. 'Agent CLI in continuous listening mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.'\n                : 'Agent CLI in single-message mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.',\n              hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n              acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                ? Exiting.',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            yargsInstance.showHelp();\n            process.exit(0);\n          }\n\n          // Try to parse as JSON, if it fails treat it as plain text message\n          let request;\n          try {\n            request = JSON.parse(trimmedInput);\n          } catch (_e) {\n            // Not JSON\n            if (!isInteractive) {\n              // In non-interactive mode, only accept JSON\n              outputStatus(\n                {\n                  type: 'error',\n                  message:\n                    'Invalid JSON input. In non-interactive mode (--no-interactive), only JSON input is accepted.',\n                  hint: 'Use --interactive to accept plain text, or provide valid JSON: {\"message\": \"your text\"}',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n            // In interactive mode, treat as plain text message\n            request = {\n              message: trimmedInput,\n            };\n          }\n\n          // Run agent mode\n          await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n        },\n      })\n      // Initialize logging early for all CLI commands\n      // This prevents debug output from appearing in CLI unless --verbose is used\n      .middleware(async (argv) => {\n        // Set verbose flag if requested\n        if (argv.verbose) {\n          Flag.setVerbose(true);\n        }\n\n        // Set dry-run flag if requested\n        if (argv['dry-run']) {\n          Flag.setDryRun(true);\n        }\n\n        // Initialize logging system\n        // - If verbose: print logs to stderr for debugging in JSON format\n        // - Otherwise: write logs to file to keep CLI output clean\n        await Log.init({\n          print: Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n          level: Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE ? 'DEBUG' : 'INFO',\n        });\n      })\n      .fail((msg, err, yargs) => {\n        // Handle errors from command handlers\n        if (err) {\n          // Check if it's a CancelledError (user pressed ESC)\n          if (UI.CancelledError.isInstance(err)) {\n            // Exit silently without showing error or help text\n            process.exit(0);\n          }\n\n          // Format other errors using FormatError\n          const formatted = FormatError(err);\n          if (formatted) {\n            outputError({\n              errorType: err.name || 'Error',\n              message: formatted,\n            });\n          } else {\n            // Fallback to default error formatting\n            outputError({\n              errorType: err.name || 'Error',\n              message: err.message || String(err),\n            });\n          }\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n\n        // Handle validation errors (msg without err)\n        if (msg) {\n          outputError({\n            errorType: 'ValidationError',\n            message: msg,\n            hint: yargs.help(),\n          });\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n      })\n      .help();\n\n    // Parse arguments (handlers will be called automatically)\n    await yargsInstance.argv;\n  } catch (error) {\n    hasError = true;\n    outputError({\n      timestamp: Date.now(),\n      errorType: error instanceof Error ? error.name : 'Error',\n      message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      stack: error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nmain();\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":202,"oldLines":9,"newStart":202,"newLines":10,"lines":["     );","     const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);","     if (!(await file.exists())) {","-      console.error(","-        `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`","-      );","+      outputError({","+        errorType: 'FileNotFound',","+        message: `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`,","+      });","       process.exit(1);","     }","     systemMessage = await file.text();"]},{"oldStart":217,"oldLines":9,"newStart":218,"newLines":10,"lines":["     );","     const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);","     if (!(await file.exists())) {","-      console.error(","-        `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`","-      );","+      outputError({","+        errorType: 'FileNotFound',","+        message: `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`,","+      });","       process.exit(1);","     }","     appendSystemMessage = await file.text();"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

join(__dirname, '../package.json');\n  const pkgContent = readFileSync(pkgPath, 'utf8');\n  pkg = JSON.parse(pkgContent);\n}\n\n// Track if any errors occurred during execution\nlet hasError = false;\n\n// Install global error handlers to ensure non-zero exit codes\nprocess.on('uncaughtException', (error) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: error.name || outputError({\n    errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n    message: reason?.message || String(reason),\n    stack: reason?.stack,\n  });\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\n/**\n * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n// outputStatus is now imported from './cli/output.ts'\n// It outputs to stdout for non-error messages, stderr for errors\n\n/**\n * Parse model configuration from argv\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { providerID, modelID }\n */\nasync function parseModelConfig(argv) {\n  // Parse model argument (handle model IDs with slashes like groq/qwen/qwen3-32b)\n  const modelParts = argv.model.split('/');\n  let providerID = modelParts[0] || 'opencode';\n  let modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/') || 'grok-code';\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('./auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified a model, use it with claude-oauth provider\n    // If not, use claude-oauth/claude-sonnet-4-5 as default\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' && modelID === 'grok-code') {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using OAuth credentials anyway.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID };\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      outputError({\n        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n        message: `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`,\n      });\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      outputError({\n        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n        message: `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`,\n      });\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runServerMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session directly\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt directly without HTTP\n    SessionPrompt.prompt({\n      sessionID,\n      parts,\n      model: {\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n      },\n      system: systemMessage,\n      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n    }).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n
