Create a lazy logger with JSON output format\n */\nexport function createLazyLogger(\n  initialTags?: Record<string, unknown>\n): LazyLogger {\n  // Determine initial log level based on verbose flag\n  const initialLevel = Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE ? LEVEL_VERBOSE : LEVEL_DISABLED;\n\n  // Create base log-lazy instance\n  const baseLog = makeLog({ level: initialLevel });\n  const tags = { ...initialTags };\n\n  // Custom output functions that format as JSON\n  const outputError = createOutput('error', tags);\n  const outputWarn = createOutput('warn', tags);\n  const outputInfo = createOutput('info', tags);\n  const outputDebug = createOutput('debug', tags);\n  const outputVerbose = createOutput('verbose', tags);\n  const outputTrace = createOutput('trace', tags);\n\n  // Create wrapper that uses JSON output\n  const wrappedLog = function (fn: () => unknown): void {\n    baseLog.info(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputInfo(result);\n      return ''; // Return empty string as the base logger just needs something\n    });\n  } as LazyLogger;\n\n  wrappedLog.error = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.error(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputError(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.warn = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.warn(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputWarn(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.info = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.info(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputInfo(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.debug = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.debug(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputDebug(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.verbose = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.verbose(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputVerbose(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.trace = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.trace(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputTrace(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  // Level management\n  wrappedLog.enableLevel = (level: LogLevel): void => {\n    baseLog.enableLevel(level);\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.disableLevel = (level: LogLevel): void => {\n    baseLog.disableLevel(level);\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.setLevel = (level: LevelPreset | number): void => {\n    const numericLevel =\n      typeof level === 'string' ? LEVEL_PRESETS[level] : level;\n\n    // Reset all levels and enable the new one\n    Object.values([\n      'error',\n      'warn',\n      'info',\n      'debug',\n      'verbose',\n      'trace',\n    ]).forEach((l) => baseLog.disableLevel(l as LogLevel));\n\n    // Enable appropriate levels based on the numeric level\n    if (numericLevel & levels.error) baseLog.enableLevel('error');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.warn) baseLog.enableLevel('warn');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.info) baseLog.enableLevel('info');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.debug) baseLog.enableLevel('debug');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.verbose) baseLog.enableLevel('verbose');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.trace) baseLog.enableLevel('trace');\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.getEnabledLevels = (): LogLevel[] => {\n    return baseLog.getEnabledLevels();\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.shouldLog = (level: LogLevel): boolean => {\n    return baseLog.shouldLog(level);\n  };\n\n  // Tag support\n  wrappedLog.tag = (key: string, value: unknown): LazyLogger => {\n    tags[key] = value;\n    return wrappedLog;\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.clone = (): LazyLogger => {\n    return createLazyLogger({ ...tags });\n  };\n\n  // Configuration\n  Object.defineProperty(wrappedLog, 'enabled', {\n    get: () => Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n    enumerable: true,\n  });\n\n  return wrappedLog;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default lazy logger instance\n * Enabled only when --verbose flag or LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE env is set\n */\nexport const lazyLog = createLazyLogger({ service: 'agent' });\n\n/**\n * Utility to update the global logger level at runtime\n * Call this after Flag.setVerbose() to sync the logger state\n */\nexport function syncLoggerWithVerboseFlag(): void {\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE) {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('verbose');\n  } else {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('disabled');\n  }\n}\n\n// Export level constants for external use\nexport { levels, LEVEL_PRESETS };\nexport type { LevelPreset };\n",

data.message;\n    logEntry.error = {\n      name: data.name,\n      message: data.message,\n      stack: data.stack,\n    };\n  } else if (typeof data === 'object' && data !== null) {\n    // Spread object properties into the log entry\n    Object.assign(logEntry, data);\n  } else {\n    logEntry.message = String(data);\n  }\n\n  return compactJsonMode\n    ? JSON.stringify(logEntry)\n    : JSON.stringify(logEntry, null, 2);\n}\n\n/**\n * Create the output function that writes to stderr\n */\nfunction createOutput(\n  level: string,\n  tags?: Record<string, unknown>\n): (data: unknown) => void {\n  return (data: unknown) => {\n    const json = formatLogEntry(level, data, tags);\n    // Use stderr to avoid interfering with stdout JSON output\n    process.stderr.write(json + '\\n');\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * LazyLogger interface extending log-lazy with JSON output\n */\nexport interface LazyLogger {\n  // Log at info level (default)\n  (fn: () => unknown): void;\n\n  // Log levels\n  error(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  warn(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  info(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  debug(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  verbose(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  trace(fn: () => unknown): void;\n\n  // Level management\n  enableLevel(level: LogLevel): void;\n  disableLevel(level: LogLevel): void;\n  setLevel(level: LevelPreset | number): void;\n  getEnabledLevels(): LogLevel[];\n  shouldLog(level: LogLevel): boolean;\n\n  // Tag support\n  tag(key: string, value: unknown): LazyLogger;\n  clone(): LazyLogger;\n\n  // Configuration\n  readonly enabled: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a lazy logger with JSON output format\n */\nexport function createLazyLogger(\n  initialTags?: Record<string, unknown>\n): LazyLogger {\n  // Determine initial log level based on verbose flag\n  const initialLevel = Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE ? LEVEL_VERBOSE : LEVEL_DISABLED;\n\n  // Create base log-lazy instance\n  const baseLog = makeLog({ level: initialLevel });\n  const tags = { ...initialTags };\n\n  // Custom output functions that format as JSON\n  const outputError = createOutput('error', tags);\n  const outputWarn = createOutput('warn', tags);\n  const outputInfo = createOutput('info', tags);\n  const outputDebug = createOutput('debug', tags);\n  const outputVerbose = createOutput('verbose', tags);\n  const outputTrace = createOutput('trace', tags);\n\n  // Create wrapper that uses JSON output\n  const wrappedLog = function (fn: () => unknown): void {\n    baseLog.info(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputInfo(result);\n      return ''; // Return empty string as the base logger just needs something\n    });\n  } as LazyLogger;\n\n  wrappedLog.error = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.error(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputError(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.warn = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.warn(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputWarn(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.info = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.info(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputInfo(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.debug = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.debug(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputDebug(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.verbose = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.verbose(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputVerbose(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.trace = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.trace(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputTrace(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  // Level management\n  wrappedLog.enableLevel = (level: LogLevel): void => {\n    baseLog.enableLevel(level);\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.disableLevel = (level: LogLevel): void => {\n    baseLog.disableLevel(level);\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.setLevel = (level: LevelPreset | number): void => {\n    const numericLevel =\n      typeof level === 'string' ? LEVEL_PRESETS[level] : level;\n\n    // Reset all levels and enable the new one\n    Object.values([\n      'error',\n      'warn',\n      'info',\n      'debug',\n      'verbose',\n      'trace',\n    ]).forEach((l) => baseLog.disableLevel(l as LogLevel));\n\n    // Enable appropriate levels based on the numeric level\n    if (numericLevel & levels.error) baseLog.enableLevel('error');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.warn) baseLog.enableLevel('warn');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.info) baseLog.enableLevel('info');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.debug) baseLog.enableLevel('debug');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.verbose) baseLog.enableLevel('verbose');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.trace) baseLog.enableLevel('trace');\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.getEnabledLevels = (): LogLevel[] => {\n    return baseLog.getEnabledLevels();\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.shouldLog = (level: LogLevel): boolean => {\n    return baseLog.shouldLog(level);\n  };\n\n  // Tag support\n  wrappedLog.tag = (key: string, value: unknown): LazyLogger => {\n    tags[key] = value;\n    return wrappedLog;\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.clone = (): LazyLogger => {\n    return createLazyLogger({ ...tags });\n  };\n\n  // Configuration\n  Object.defineProperty(wrappedLog, 'enabled', {\n    get: () => Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n    enumerable: true,\n  });\n\n  return wrappedLog;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default lazy logger instance\n * Enabled only when --verbose flag or LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE env is set\n */\nexport const lazyLog = createLazyLogger({ service: 'agent' });\n\n/**\n * Utility to update the global logger level at runtime\n * Call this after Flag.setVerbose() to sync the logger state\n */\nexport function syncLoggerWithVerboseFlag(): void {\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE) {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('verbose');\n  } else {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('disabled');\n  }\n}\n\n// Export level constants for external use\nexport { levels, LEVEL_PRESETS };\nexport type { LevelPreset };\n",

    "originalFile": "import path from 'path';\nimport fs from 'fs/promises';\nimport { Global } from '../global';\nimport z from 'zod';\nimport makeLog, { levels } from 'log-lazy';\nimport { Flag } from '../flag/flag.ts';\n\n/**\n * Logging module with JSON output and lazy evaluation support.\n *\n * Features:\n * - JSON formatted output: All logs use { \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"...\", ... } structure\n * - Lazy evaluation: Use lazy() methods to defer expensive computations\n * - Level control: Respects --verbose flag and log level settings\n * - File logging: Writes to file when not in verbose/print mode\n * - Stdout by default: In print mode, logs go to stdout (not stderr) to follow Unix conventions\n *\n * The JSON format with `type` field ensures all output is consistent with other CLI output.\n */\nexport namespace Log {\n  export const Level = z\n    .enum(['DEBUG', 'INFO', 'WARN', 'ERROR'])\n    .meta({ ref: 'LogLevel', description: 'Log level' });\n  export type Level = z.infer<typeof Level>;\n\n  const levelPriority: Record<Level, number> = {\n    DEBUG: 0,\n    INFO: 1,\n    WARN: 2,\n    ERROR: 3,\n  };\n\n  let level: Level = 'INFO';\n  let jsonOutput = false; // Whether to output JSON format (enabled in verbose mode)\n  let compactJsonOutput = false; // Whether to use compact JSON (single line)\n\n  function shouldLog(input: Level): boolean {\n    return levelPriority[input] >= levelPriority[level];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Logger interface with support for both immediate and lazy logging.\n   *\n   * All logging methods accept either:\n   * - A message string/object/Error with optional extra data\n   * - A function that returns the data to log (lazy evaluation)\n   *\n   * Lazy logging: The function is only called if logging is enabled for that level,\n   * avoiding expensive computations when logs are disabled.\n   */\n  export type Logger = {\n    // Unified logging - supports both immediate and lazy (callback) styles\n    debug(\n      message?: any | (() => { message?: string; [key: string]: any }),\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): void;\n    info(\n      message?: any | (() => { message?: string; [key: string]: any }),\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): void;\n    error(\n      message?: any | (() => { message?: string; [key: string]: any }),\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): void;\n    warn(\n      message?: any | (() => { message?: string; [key: string]: any }),\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): void;\n\n    tag(key: string, value: string): Logger;\n    clone(): Logger;\n    time(\n      message: string,\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): {\n      stop(): void;\n      [Symbol.dispose](): void;\n    };\n  };\n\n  const loggers = new Map<string, Logger>();\n\n  export const Default = create({ service: 'default' });\n\n  export interface Options {\n    print: boolean;\n    dev?: boolean;\n    level?: Level;\n    compactJson?: boolean;\n  }\n\n  let logpath = '';\n  export function file() {\n    return logpath;\n  }\n  // Default to stdout for log output (following Unix conventions: stdout for data, stderr for errors)\n  let write = (msg: any) => Bun.stdout.write(msg);\n\n  // Initialize log-lazy for controlling lazy log execution\n  let lazyLogInstance = makeLog({ level: 0 }); // Start disabled\n\n   export async function init(options: Options) {\n     if (options.level) level = options.level;\n     if (options.compactJson !== undefined) compactJsonOutput = options.compactJson;\n     cleanup(Global.Path.log);\n\n     // Always use JSON output format for logs\n     jsonOutput = true;\n\n     // Configure lazy logging level based on verbose flag\n     if (Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE || options.print) {\n       // Enable all levels for lazy logging when verbose\n       lazyLogInstance = makeLog({\n         level: levels.debug | levels.info | levels.warn | levels.error,\n       });\n     } else {\n       // Disable lazy logging when not verbose\n       lazyLogInstance = makeLog({ level: 0 });\n     }\n\n     // Always output logs to stdout as JSON (following the issue requirement)\n     // Logs should be formatted as JSON output by default\n     write = (msg: any) => Bun.stdout.write(msg);\n\n     // Still create log file for debugging if needed, but primary output is stdout\n     logpath = path.join(\n       Global.Path.log,\n       options.dev\n         ? 'dev.log'\n         : new Date().toISOString().split('.')[0].replace(/:/g, '') + '.log'\n     );\n     const logfile = Bun.file(logpath);\n     await fs.truncate(logpath).catch(() => {});\n     const writer = logfile.writer();\n     // Also write to file for debugging purposes\n     const originalWrite = write;\n     write = async (msg: any) => {\n       originalWrite(msg);\n       const num = writer.write(msg);\n       writer.flush();\n       return num;\n     };\n   }\n\n  async function cleanup(dir: string) {\n    const glob = new Bun.Glob('????-??-??T??????.log');\n    const files = await Array.fromAsync(\n      glob.scan({\n        cwd: dir,\n        absolute: true,\n      })\n    );\n    if (files.length <= 5) return;\n\n    const filesToDelete = files.slice(0, -10);\n    await Promise.all(\n      filesToDelete.map((file) => fs.unlink(file).catch(() => {}))\n    );\n  }\n\n  function formatError(error: Error, depth = 0): string {\n    const result = error.message;\n    return error.cause instanceof Error && depth < 10\n      ? result + ' Caused by: ' + formatError(error.cause, depth + 1)\n      : result;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format log entry as JSON object with { \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"...\", ... } structure\n   * This flattened format is consistent with other CLI JSON output.\n   */\n  function formatJson(\n    logLevel: Level,\n    message: any,\n    tags: Record<string, any>,\n    extra?: Record<string, any>\n  ): string {\n    const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();\n    const logEntry: Record<string, any> = {\n      type: 'log',\n      level: logLevel.toLowerCase(),\n      timestamp,\n      ...tags,\n      ...extra,\n    };\n\n    if (message !== undefined && message !== null) {\n      if (typeof message === 'string') {\n        logEntry.message = message;\n      } else if (message instanceof Error) {\n        logEntry.message = message.message;\n        logEntry.error = {\n          name: message.name,\n          message: message.message,\n          stack: message.stack,\n        };\n      } else if (typeof message === 'object') {\n        Object.assign(logEntry, message);\n      } else {\n        logEntry.message = String(message);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Use compact or pretty format based on configuration\n    return compactJsonOutput\n      ? JSON.stringify(logEntry)\n      : JSON.stringify(logEntry, null, 2);\n  }\n\n  let last = Date.now();\n  export function create(tags?: Record<string, any>) {\n    tags = tags || {};\n\n    const service = tags['service'];\n    if (service && typeof service === 'string') {\n      const cached = loggers.get(service);\n      if (cached) {\n        return cached;\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Legacy format for file logging (backward compatibility)\n    function buildLegacy(message: any, extra?: Record<string, any>) {\n      const prefix = Object.entries({\n        ...tags,\n        ...extra,\n      })\n        .filter(([_, value]) => value !== undefined && value !== null)\n        .map(([key, value]) => {\n          const prefix = `${key}=`;\n          if (value instanceof Error) return prefix + formatError(value);\n          if (typeof value === 'object') return prefix + JSON.stringify(value);\n          return prefix + value;\n        })\n        .join(' ');\n      const next = new Date();\n      const diff = next.getTime() - last;\n      last = next.getTime();\n      return (\n        [next.toISOString().split('.')[0], '+' + diff + 'ms', prefix, message]\n          .filter(Boolean)\n          .join(' ') + '\\n'\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Choose output format based on jsonOutput flag\n    function output(\n      logLevel: Level,\n      message: any,\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ) {\n      if (jsonOutput) {\n        // Use our custom JSON formatting for { log: { ... } } format\n        write(formatJson(logLevel, message, tags || {}, extra) + '\\n');\n      } else {\n        write(logLevel.padEnd(5) + ' ' + buildLegacy(message, extra));\n      }\n    }\n\n    const result: Logger = {\n      debug(message?: any, extra?: Record<string, any>) {\n        if (!shouldLog('DEBUG')) return;\n\n        // Check if message is a function (lazy logging)\n        if (typeof message === 'function') {\n          lazyLogInstance.debug(() => {\n            const data = message();\n            const { message: msg, ...extraData } = data;\n            output('DEBUG', msg, extraData);\n            return '';\n          });\n        } else {\n          output('DEBUG', message, extra);\n        }\n      },\n      info(message?: any, extra?: Record<string, any>) {\n        if (!shouldLog('INFO')) return;\n\n        // Check if message is a function // Whether to output JSON format (enabled in verbose mode)\n  let compactJsonOutput = Flag.COMPACT_JSON; // Whether to use compact JSON (single line)\n\n  function shouldLog(input: Level): boolean {\n    return levelPriority[input] >= levelPriority[level];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Logger interface with support for both immediate and lazy logging.\n   *\n   * All logging methods accept either:\n   * - A message string/object/Error with optional extra data\n   * - A function that returns the data to log (lazy evaluation)\n   *\n   * Lazy logging: The function is only called if logging is enabled for that level,\n   * avoiding expensive computations when logs are disabled.\n   */\n  export type Logger = {\n    // Unified logging - supports both immediate and lazy (callback) styles\n    debug(\n      message?: any | (() => { message?: string; [key: string]: any }),\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): void;\n    warn(\n      message?: any | (() => { message?: string; [key: string]: any }),\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): void;\n\n    tag(key: string, value: string): Logger;\n    clone(): Logger;\n    time(\n      message: string,\n      extra?: Record<string, any>\n    ): {\n      stop(): void;\n      [Symbol.dispose](): void;\n    };\n  };\n\n  const loggers = new Map<string, Logger>();\n\n  export const Default = create({ service: 'default' });\n\n  export interface Options {\n    print: boolean;\n    dev?: boolean;\n    level?: Level;\n    compactJson?: boolean;\n  }\n\n  let logpath = '';\n  export function file() {\n    return logpath;\n  }\n  // Default to stdout for log output (following Unix conventions: stdout for data, stderr for errors)\n  let write = (msg: any) => Bun.stdout.write(msg);\n\n  // Initialize log-lazy for controlling lazy log execution\n  let lazyLogInstance = makeLog({ level: 0 }); '\\n');\n      } else {\n        write(logLevel.padEnd(5) + ' ' + buildLegacy(message, extra));\n      }\n    }\n\n    const result: Logger = {\n      debug(message?: any, extra?: Record<string, any>)     "originalFile": "import makeLog, { levels, LogLevel } from 'log-lazy';\nimport { Flag } from '../flag/flag.ts';\n\n/**\n * JSON Lazy Logger\n *\n * Implements lazy logging pattern using log-lazy library.\n * All log output is JSON formatted with { \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"...\", ... } structure\n * for consistent parsing with other CLI JSON output.\n *\n * Key features:\n * - Lazy evaluation: log arguments are only computed if logging is enabled\n * - JSON output: all logs are parsable JSON with `type: \"log\"` field\n * - Stdout output: logs go to stdout (not stderr) following Unix conventions\n * - Level control: logs respect --verbose flag and LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE env\n * - Type-safe: full TypeScript support\n *\n * Usage:\n *   import { lazyLog } from './util/log-lazy.ts';\n *\n *   // Simple string message\n *   lazyLog.info(() => 'Starting process');\n *\n *   // Object data (preferred - avoids expensive JSON.stringify when disabled)\n *   lazyLog.debug(() => ({ action: 'fetch', url: someUrl }));\n *\n *   // Complex computed message\n *   lazyLog.verbose(() => `Processed ${items.length} items: ${JSON.stringify(items)}`);\n */\n\n// Custom log levels using bit flags for fine-grained control\nconst LEVEL_DISABLED = 0;\nconst LEVEL_ERROR = levels.error;\nconst LEVEL_WARN = levels.warn | LEVEL_ERROR;\nconst LEVEL_INFO = levels.info | LEVEL_WARN;\nconst LEVEL_DEBUG = levels.debug | LEVEL_INFO;\nconst LEVEL_VERBOSE = levels.verbose | LEVEL_DEBUG;\nconst LEVEL_TRACE = levels.trace | LEVEL_VERBOSE;\n\n// Map of preset level configurations\nconst LEVEL_PRESETS = {\n  disabled: LEVEL_DISABLED,\n  error: LEVEL_ERROR,\n  warn: LEVEL_WARN,\n  info: LEVEL_INFO,\n  debug: LEVEL_DEBUG,\n  verbose: LEVEL_VERBOSE,\n  trace: LEVEL_TRACE,\n  // Convenience aliases\n  production: LEVEL_WARN,\n  development: LEVEL_DEBUG,\n} as const;\n\ntype LevelPreset = keyof typeof LEVEL_PRESETS;\n\n// Compact JSON mode (can be set at runtime)\nlet compactJsonMode = false;\n\n/**\n * Set compact JSON output mode\n */\nexport function setCompactJson(compact: boolean): void {\n  compactJsonMode = compact;\n}\n\n/**\n * Format a log entry as JSON object with { \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"...\", ... } structure\n * This flattened format is consistent with other CLI JSON output.\n */\nfunction formatLogEntry(\n  level: string,\n  data: unknown,\n  tags?: Record<string, unknown>\n): string {\n  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();\n  const logEntry: Record<string, unknown> = {\n    type: 'log',\n    level,\n    timestamp,\n    ...tags,\n  };\n\n  // Handle different data types\n  if (typeof data === 'string') {\n    logEntry.message = data;\n  } else if (data instanceof Error) {\n    logEntry.message = data.message;\n    logEntry.error = {\n      name: data.name,\n      message: data.message,\n      stack: data.stack,\n    };\n  } else if (typeof data === 'object' && data !== null) {\n    // Spread object properties into the log entry\n    Object.assign(logEntry, data);\n  } else {\n    logEntry.message = String(data);\n  }\n\n  return compactJsonMode\n    ? JSON.stringify(logEntry)\n    : JSON.stringify(logEntry, null, 2);\n}\n\n/**\n * Create the output function that writes to stdout\n * Using stdout follows Unix conventions (stdout for data, stderr for errors)\n */\nfunction createOutput(\n  level: string,\n  tags?: Record<string, unknown>\n): (data: unknown) => void {\n  return (data: unknown) => {\n    const json = formatLogEntry(level, data, tags);\n    // Use stdout following Unix conventions\n    process.stdout.write(json + '\\n');\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * LazyLogger interface extending log-lazy with JSON output\n */\nexport interface LazyLogger {\n  // Log at info level (default)\n  (fn: () => unknown): void;\n\n  // Log levels\n  error(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  warn(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  info(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  debug(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  verbose(fn: () => unknown): void;\n  trace(fn: () => unknown): void;\n\n  // Level management\n  enableLevel(level: LogLevel): void;\n  disableLevel(level: LogLevel): void;\n  setLevel(level: LevelPreset | number): void;\n  getEnabledLevels(): LogLevel[];\n  shouldLog(level: LogLevel): boolean;\n\n  // Tag support\n  tag(key: string, value: unknown): LazyLogger;\n  clone(): LazyLogger;\n\n  // Configuration\n  readonly enabled: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a lazy logger with JSON output format\n */\nexport function createLazyLogger(\n  initialTags?: Record<string, unknown>\n): LazyLogger {\n  // Determine initial log level based on verbose flag\n  const initialLevel = Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE ? LEVEL_VERBOSE : LEVEL_DISABLED;\n\n  // Create base log-lazy instance\n  const baseLog = makeLog({ level: initialLevel });\n  const tags = { ...initialTags };\n\n  // Custom output functions that format as JSON\n  const outputError = createOutput('error', tags);\n  const outputWarn = createOutput('warn', tags);\n  const outputInfo = createOutput('info', tags);\n  const outputDebug = createOutput('debug', tags);\n  const outputVerbose = createOutput('verbose', tags);\n  const outputTrace = createOutput('trace', tags);\n\n  // Create wrapper that uses JSON output\n  const wrappedLog = function (fn: () => unknown): void {\n    baseLog.info(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputInfo(result);\n      return ''; // Return empty string as the base logger just needs something\n    });\n  } as LazyLogger;\n\n  wrappedLog.error = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.error(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputError(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.warn = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.warn(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputWarn(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.info = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.info(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputInfo(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.debug = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.debug(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputDebug(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.verbose = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.verbose(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputVerbose(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.trace = (fn: () => unknown): void => {\n    baseLog.trace(() => {\n      const result = fn();\n      outputTrace(result);\n      return '';\n    });\n  };\n\n  // Level management\n  wrappedLog.enableLevel = (level: LogLevel): void => {\n    baseLog.enableLevel(level);\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.disableLevel = (level: LogLevel): void => {\n    baseLog.disableLevel(level);\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.setLevel = (level: LevelPreset | number): void => {\n    const numericLevel =\n      typeof level === 'string' ? LEVEL_PRESETS[level] : level;\n\n    // Reset all levels and enable the new one\n    Object.values([\n      'error',\n      'warn',\n      'info',\n      'debug',\n      'verbose',\n      'trace',\n    ]).forEach((l) => baseLog.disableLevel(l as LogLevel));\n\n    // Enable appropriate levels based on the numeric level\n    if (numericLevel & levels.error) baseLog.enableLevel('error');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.warn) baseLog.enableLevel('warn');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.info) baseLog.enableLevel('info');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.debug) baseLog.enableLevel('debug');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.verbose) baseLog.enableLevel('verbose');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.trace) baseLog.enableLevel('trace');\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.getEnabledLevels = (): LogLevel[] => {\n    return baseLog.getEnabledLevels();\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.shouldLog = (level: LogLevel): boolean => {\n    return baseLog.shouldLog(level);\n  };\n\n  // Tag support\n  wrappedLog.tag = (key: string, value: unknown): LazyLogger => {\n    tags[key] = value;\n    return wrappedLog;\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.clone = (): LazyLogger => {\n    return createLazyLogger({ ...tags });\n  };\n\n  // Configuration\n  Object.defineProperty(wrappedLog, 'enabled', {\n    get: () => Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n    enumerable: true,\n  });\n\n  return wrappedLog;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default lazy logger instance\n * Enabled only when --verbose flag or LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE env is set\n */\nexport const lazyLog = createLazyLogger({ service: 'agent' });\n\n/**\n * Utility to update the global logger level at runtime\n * Call this after Flag.setVerbose() to sync the logger state\n */\nexport function syncLoggerWithVerboseFlag(): void {\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE) {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('verbose');\n  } else {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('disabled');\n  }\n}\n\n// Export level constants for external use\nexport { levels, LEVEL_PRESETS };\nexport type { LevelPreset };\n",

LEVEL_PRESETS[level] : level;\n\n    // Reset all levels and enable the new one\n    Object.values([\n      'error',\n      'warn',\n      'info',\n      'debug',\n      'verbose',\n      'trace',\n    ]).forEach((l) => baseLog.disableLevel(l as LogLevel));\n\n    // Enable appropriate levels based on the numeric level\n    if (numericLevel & levels.error) baseLog.enableLevel('error');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.warn) baseLog.enableLevel('warn');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.info) baseLog.enableLevel('info');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.debug) baseLog.enableLevel('debug');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.verbose) baseLog.enableLevel('verbose');\n    if (numericLevel & levels.trace) baseLog.enableLevel('trace');\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.getEnabledLevels = (): LogLevel[] => {\n    return baseLog.getEnabledLevels();\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.shouldLog = (level: LogLevel): boolean => {\n    return baseLog.shouldLog(level);\n  };\n\n  // Tag support\n  wrappedLog.tag = (key: string, value: unknown): LazyLogger => {\n    tags[key] = value;\n    return wrappedLog;\n  };\n\n  wrappedLog.clone = (): LazyLogger => {\n    return createLazyLogger({ ...tags });\n  };\n\n  // Configuration\n  Object.defineProperty(wrappedLog, 'enabled', {\n    get: () => Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE,\n    enumerable: true,\n  });\n\n  return wrappedLog;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default lazy logger instance\n * Enabled only when --verbose flag or LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE env is set\n */\nexport const lazyLog = createLazyLogger({ service: 'agent' });\n\n/**\n * Utility to update the global logger level at runtime\n * Call this after Flag.setVerbose() to sync the logger state\n */\nexport function syncLoggerWithVerboseFlag(): void {\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE) {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('verbose');\n  } else {\n    lazyLog.setLevel('disabled');\n  }\n}\n\n// Export level constants for external use\nexport { levels, LEVEL_PRESETS };\nexport type { LevelPreset };\n",

*/\nexport function writeStderr(\n  message: OutputMessage,\n  compact?: boolean\n): void {\n  const json = formatJson(message, compact);\n  process.stderr.write(json + EOL);\n}\n\n/**\n * Output a message to the appropriate stream based on type\n * - Errors go to stderr\n * - Everything else goes to stdout\n *\n * @param message - The message object to output\n * @param compact - Override the Flag.COMPACT_JSON;\n  return useCompact\n    ? Flag.COMPACT_JSON;\n  return useCompact\n    ? JSON.stringify(message)\n    : JSON.stringify(message, null, 2);\n}\n\n/**\n * Write a message to stdout (for normal output)\n * @param message - The message object to output\n * @param compact -     "originalFile": "/**\n * Continuous stdin mode for the Agent CLI.\n * Keeps the session alive and processes messages as they arrive.\n */\n\nimport { Server } from '../server/server.ts';\nimport { Instance } from '../project/instance.ts';\nimport { Bus } from '../bus/index.ts';\nimport { Session } from '../session/index.ts';\nimport { SessionPrompt } from '../session/prompt.ts';\nimport { createEventHandler } from '../json-standard/index.ts';\nimport { createContinuousStdinReader } from './input-queue.js';\nimport { Log } from '../util/log.ts';\nimport { outputStatus, outputError } from './output.ts';\n\n// Shared error tracking\nlet hasError = false;\n\n/**\n * Set error state\n * @param {boolean} value - Error state value\n */\nexport function setHasError(value) {\n  hasError = value;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get error state\n * @returns {boolean}\n */\nexport function getHasError() {\n  return hasError;\n}\n\n// outputStatus is now imported from './output.ts'\n// It outputs to stdout for non-error messages, stderr for errors\n\n// Logger for resume operations\nconst log = Log.create({ service: 'resume' });\n\n/**\n * Resolve the session to use based on --resume, --continue, and --no-fork options.\n * Returns the session ID to use, handling forking as needed.\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @param {boolean} compactJson - Whether to use compact JSON output\n * @returns {Promise<{sessionID: string, wasResumed: boolean, wasForked: boolean} | null>} - Session info or null if new session should be created\n * @export\n */\nexport async function resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson) {\n  const resumeSessionID = argv.resume;\n  const shouldContinue = argv.continue === true;\n  const noFork = argv['no-fork'] === true;\n\n  // If neither --resume nor --continue is specified, return null to create new session\n  if (!resumeSessionID && !shouldContinue) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  let targetSessionID = resumeSessionID;\n\n  // If --continue is specified, find the most recent session\n  if (shouldContinue && !targetSessionID) {\n    let mostRecentSession = null;\n    let mostRecentTime = 0;\n\n    for await (const session of Session.list()) {\n      // Skip child sessions (those with parentID) - find top-level sessions only\n      if (session.parentID) {\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      if (session.time.updated > mostRecentTime) {\n        mostRecentTime = session.time.updated;\n        mostRecentSession = session;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (!mostRecentSession) {\n      outputError(\n        {\n          errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n          message:\n            'No existing sessions found to continue. Start a new session first.',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    targetSessionID = mostRecentSession.id;\n    log.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Found most recent session to continue',\n      sessionID: targetSessionID,\n      title: mostRecentSession.title,\n    }));\n  }\n\n  // Verify the session exists\n  let existingSession;\n  try {\n    existingSession = await Session.get(targetSessionID);\n  } catch (_error) {\n    // Session.get throws an error when the session doesn't exist\n    outputError(\n      {\n        errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n        message: `Session not found: ${targetSessionID}`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  if (!existingSession) {\n    outputError(\n      {\n        errorType: 'SessionNotFound',\n        message: `Session not found: ${targetSessionID}`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  log.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Resuming session',\n    sessionID: targetSessionID,\n    title: existingSession.title,\n    noFork,\n  }));\n\n  // If --no-fork is specified, continue in the same session\n  if (noFork) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'status',\n        mode: 'resume',\n        message: `Continuing session without forking: ${targetSessionID}`,\n        sessionID: targetSessionID,\n        title: existingSession.title,\n        forked: false,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n\n    return {\n      sessionID: targetSessionID,\n      wasResumed: true,\n      wasForked: false,\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Fork the session to a new UUID (default behavior)\n  const forkedSession = await Session.fork({\n    sessionID: targetSessionID,\n  });\n\n  outputStatus(\n    {\n      type: 'status',\n      mode: 'resume',\n      message: `Forked session ${targetSessionID} to new session: ${forkedSession.id}`,\n      originalSessionID: targetSessionID,\n      sessionID: forkedSession.id,\n      title: forkedSession.title,\n      forked: true,\n    },\n    compactJson\n  );\n\n  log.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Forked session',\n    originalSessionID: targetSessionID,\n    newSessionID: forkedSession.id,\n  }));\n\n  return {\n    sessionID: forkedSession.id,\n    wasResumed: true,\n    wasForked: true,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Run server mode with continuous stdin input\n * Keeps the session alive and processes messages as they arrive\n */\nexport async function runContinuousServerMode(\n  argv,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n  const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n  let stdinReader = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Track if we're currently processing a message\n    let isProcessing = false;\n    const pendingMessages = [];\n\n    // Process messages from the queue\n    const processMessage = async (message) => {\n      if (isProcessing) {\n        pendingMessages.push(message);\n        return;\n      }\n\n      isProcessing = true;\n\n      // Output input confirmation in JSON format\n      outputInput(\n        {\n          raw: message.raw || message.message,\n          parsed: message,\n          format: message.format || 'text',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n\n      const messageText = message.message || 'hi';\n      const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: messageText }];\n\n      // Create a promise to wait for this message to complete\n      const messagePromise = new Promise((resolve) => {\n        const checkIdle = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n          if (\n            event.type === 'session.idle' &&\n            event.properties.sessionID === sessionID\n          ) {\n            checkIdle();\n            resolve();\n          }\n        });\n      });\n\n      // Send message\n      fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({\n            parts,\n            model: { providerID, modelID },\n            system: systemMessage,\n            appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n          }),\n        }\n      ).catch((error) => {\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n        });\n      });\n\n      await messagePromise;\n      isProcessing = false;\n\n      // Process next pending message if any\n      if (pendingMessages.length > 0) {\n        const nextMessage = pendingMessages.shift();\n        processMessage(nextMessage);\n      }\n    };\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    unsub = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n      if (event.type === 'message.part.updated') {\n        const part = event.properties.part;\n        if (part.sessionID !== sessionID) {\n          return;\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-start') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_start',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-finish') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_finish',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'text' && part.time?.end) {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'text',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'tool') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'tool_use',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (event.type === 'session.error') {\n        const props = event.properties;\n        if (props.sessionID !== sessionID || !props.error) {\n          return;\n        }\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: props.error,\n        });\n      }\n    });\n\n    // Create continuous stdin reader\n    stdinReader = createContinuousStdinReader({\n      interactive: isInteractive,\n      autoMerge,\n      onMessage: (message) => {\n        processMessage(message);\n      },\n    });\n\n    // Wait for stdin to end (EOF or close)\n    await new Promise((resolve) => {\n      const checkRunning = setInterval(() => {\n        if (!stdinReader.isRunning()) {\n          clearInterval(checkRunning);\n          // Wait for any pending messages to complete\n          const waitForPending = () => {\n            if (!isProcessing && pendingMessages.length === 0) {\n              resolve();\n            } else {\n              setTimeout(waitForPending, 100);\n            }\n          };\n          waitForPending();\n        }\n      }, 100);\n\n      // Also handle SIGINT\n      process.on('SIGINT', () => {\n        outputStatus(\n          {\n            type: 'status',\n            message: 'Received SIGINT. Shutting down...',\n          },\n          compactJson\n        );\n        clearInterval(checkRunning);\n        resolve();\n      });\n    });\n  } finally {\n    if (stdinReader) {\n      stdinReader.stop();\n    }\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Run direct mode with continuous stdin input\n * Keeps the session alive and processes messages as they arrive\n */\nexport async function runContinuousDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n  const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n\n  let unsub = null;\n  let stdinReader = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Track if we're currently processing a message\n    let isProcessing = false;\n    const pendingMessages = [];\n\n    // Process messages from the queue\n    const processMessage = async (message) => {\n      if (isProcessing) {\n        pendingMessages.push(message);\n        return;\n      }\n\n      isProcessing = true;\n\n      // Output input confirmation in JSON format\n      outputInput(\n        {\n          raw: message.raw || message.message,\n          parsed: message,\n          format: message.format || 'text',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n\n      const messageText = message.message || 'hi';\n      const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: messageText }];\n\n      // Create a promise to wait for this message to complete\n      const messagePromise = new Promise((resolve) => {\n        const checkIdle = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n          if (\n            event.type === 'session.idle' &&\n            event.properties.sessionID === sessionID\n          ) {\n            checkIdle();\n            resolve();\n          }\n        });\n      });\n\n      // Send message directly\n      SessionPrompt.prompt({\n        sessionID,\n        parts,\n        model: { providerID, modelID },\n        system: systemMessage,\n        appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n      }).catch((error) => {\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n          error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n        });\n      });\n\n      await messagePromise;\n      isProcessing = false;\n\n      // Process next pending message if any\n      if (pendingMessages.length > 0) {\n        const nextMessage = pendingMessages.shift();\n        processMessage(nextMessage);\n      }\n    };\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    unsub = Bus.subscribeAll((event) => {\n      if (event.type === 'message.part.updated') {\n        const part = event.properties.part;\n        if (part.sessionID !== sessionID) {\n          return;\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-start') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_start',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n\n        if (part.type === 'step-finish') {\n          eventHandler.output({\n            type: 'step_finish',\n            timestamp: Date.now(),\n            sessionID,\n        sessionID,\n            part,\n          });\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (event.type === 'session.error') {\n        const props = event.properties;\n        if (props.sessionID !== sessionID || !props.error) {\n          return;\n        }\n        hasError = true;\n        eventHandler.output({\n          type: 'error',\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n          sessionID,\n Issue #131: \"Agent CLI outputs stderr instead of stdout\"\n   - The CLI sends ALL output to stderr instead of stdout\n   - Requirements: Output everything except errors to stdout, add `type` field to all JSON, flatten log statements, make log formatting configurable\n\n3. **Investigation Phase**:\n   - `js/src/util/log.ts:93`: Default log writer uses `Bun.stderr.write(msg)`\n   - `js/src/util/log-lazy.ts:99-100`: Lazy logging uses `process.stderr.write()`\n\n5. **Implementation Work**:\n   - Created case study documentation at `docs/case-studies/issue-131/README.md`\n   - Created centralized output module at `js/src/cli/output.ts`\n   - Updated `js/src/index.js` to use the new output module\n   - Updated `js/src/cli/continuous-mode.js` to use the new output module\n   - Updated `js/src/util/log.ts` to output to stdout and use flattened format\n   - Updated `js/src/util/log-lazy.ts` similarly\n   - Added `COMPACT_JSON` flag support in `js/src/flag/flag.ts`\n\n6. **Key Changes Made**:\n   - Log format changed from `{ \"log\": { \"level\": \"info\", ... } }` to `{ \"type\": \"log\", \"level\": \"info\", ... }`\n   - Output routing: stdout for normal output, stderr for errors only\n   - Added `AGENT_CLI_COMPACT` environment variable support for NDJSON format\n   - Changed `Flag.COMPACT_JSON` from a constant to a function to check env vars at runtime\n\n7. **Testing Issues**:\n   - Tests expect NDJSON format (one JSON per line)\n   - The `AGENT_CLI_COMPACT` env var wasn't being respected at runtime\n   - Fixed by making `Flag.COMPACT_JSON()` a function instead of a value\n\n8. **Most Recent Error**:\n   - `outputInput is not defined` error in continuous-mode.js\n   - Fixed by adding `outputInput` to the import statement\n\n9. **Current state**: Just fixed the import for `outputInput` in continuous-mode.js\n\nSummary:\n1. Primary Request and Intent:\n   The user requested solving GitHub issue #131 for the link-assistant/agent repository. The issue states that the Agent CLI outputs everything to stderr instead of stdout. The requirements are:\n   - Output everything except errors to stdout by default\n   - All JSON output must have a `type` field\n   - Flatten log statements from `{ \"log\": { ... } }` to `{ \"type\": \"log\", ... }`\n   - Make log formatting configurable (compact/pretty)\n   - Create case study documentation in `./docs/case-studies/issue-131`\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - Unix conventions: stdout for data, stderr for errors only\n   - NDJSON (Newline-Delimited JSON) format for compact output\n   - Bun runtime and TypeScript\n   - JSON output formatting (compact vs pretty-printed)\n   - Environment variable-based configuration (`AGENT_CLI_COMPACT`)\n   - Module system with centralized output handling\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n\n   - **`js/src/cli/output.ts`** (NEW FILE)\n     - Centralized output module for consistent JSON formatting\n     - Routes stdout for normal output, stderr for errors\n     - All output includes `type` field\n     ```typescript\n     import { EOL } from 'os';\n     import { Flag } from '../flag/flag.ts';\n     \n     export type OutputType = 'status' | 'error' | 'warning' | 'log' | 'step_start' | 'step_finish' | 'text' | 'tool_use' | 'result' | 'init' | 'message' | 'tool_result' | 'input';\n     \n     let globalCompactJson: boolean | null = null;\n     \n     export function isCompactJson(): boolean {\n       if (globalCompactJson !== null) return globalCompactJson;\n       return Flag.COMPACT_JSON();\n     }\n     \n     export function formatJson(message: OutputMessage, compact?: boolean): string {\n       const useCompact = compact ?? isCompactJson();\n       return useCompact ? JSON.stringify(message) : JSON.stringify(message, null, 2);\n     }\n     \n     export function outputError(error: {...}, compact?: boolean): void {\n       const message: OutputMessage = { type: 'error', ...error };\n       writeStderr(message, compact);  // Errors go to stderr\n     }\n     \n     export function outputStatus(status: {...}, compact?: boolean): void {\n       const message: OutputMessage = { type: status.type || 'status', ...status };\n       output(message, compact);  // Status goes to stdout\n     }\n     ```\n\n   - **`js/src/index.js`**\n     - Main CLI entry point\n     - Updated to use centralized output module\n     - Removed local `outputStatus` function\n     - Updated error handlers to use `outputError`\n     ```javascript\n     import { outputStatus, outputError, setCompactJson } from './cli/output.ts';\n     \n     process.on('uncaughtException', (error) => {\n       hasError = true;\n       outputError({\n         errorType: error.name || 'UncaughtException',\n         message: error.message,\n         stack: error.stack,\n       });\n       process.exit(1);\n     });\n     ```\n\n   - **`js/src/cli/continuous-mode.js`**\n     - Updated imports to use centralized output module\n     - Most recently added `outputInput` to imports\n     ```javascript\n     import { outputStatus, outputError, outputInput } from './output.ts';\n     ```\n\n   - **`js/src/util/log.ts`**\n     - Changed default output to stdout\n     - Changed log format from `{ log: {...} }` to `{ type: \"log\", ... }`\n     - Added compact JSON support\n     ```typescript\n     let compactJsonOutput = Flag.COMPACT_JSON();\n     \n     function formatJson(logLevel: Level, message: any, tags: Record<string, any>, extra?: Record<string, any>): string {\n       const logEntry: Record<string, any> = {\n         type: 'log',  // Flattened format\n         level: logLevel.toLowerCase(),\n         timestamp,\n         ...tags,\n         ...extra,\n       };\n       const useCompact = compactJsonOutput || Flag.COMPACT_JSON();\n       return useCompact ? JSON.stringify(logEntry) : JSON.stringify(logEntry, null, 2);\n     }\n     ```\n\n   - **`js/src/flag/flag.ts`**\n     - Added `COMPACT_JSON` as a function (not constant) to check env vars at runtime\n     ```typescript\n     let _compactJson: boolean | null = null;\n     \n     export function COMPACT_JSON(): boolean {\n       if (_compactJson !== null) return _compactJson;\n       return (\n         truthy('AGENT_CLI_COMPACT') ||\n         truthyCompat('LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_COMPACT_JSON', 'OPENCODE_COMPACT_JSON')\n       );\n     }\n     \n     export function setCompactJson(value: boolean) {\n       _compactJson = value;\n     }\n     ```\n\n   - **`docs/case-studies/issue-131/README.md`** (NEW FILE)\n     - Case study documentation for the issue\n     - Contains root cause analysis, solution design, files changed\n\n4. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n// outputStatus is now imported from './cli/output.ts'\n// It outputs to stdout for non-error messages, stderr for errors\n\n/**\n * Parse model configuration from argv\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { providerID, modelID }\n */\nasync function parseModelConfig(argv) {\n  // Parse model argument (handle model IDs with slashes like groq/qwen/qwen3-32b)\n  const modelParts = argv.model.split('/');\n  let providerID = modelParts[0] || 'opencode';\n  let modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/') || 'grok-code';\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('./auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'error',\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified a model, use it with claude-oauth provider\n    // If not, use claude-oauth/claude-sonnet-4-5 as default\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' && modelID === 'grok-code') {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using OAuth credentials anyway.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID };\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      outputError({\n        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n        message: `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`,\n      });\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      outputError({\n        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n        message: `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`,\n      });\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runServerMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          request,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (Flag.OPENCODE_DRY_RUN) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  const { providerID, modelID } = await parseModelConfig(argv);\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: async () => {\n      if (argv.server) {\n        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      } else {\n        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard\n        );\n      }\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const createRes = await fetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session directly\n    const message = request.message || providerID,\n        modelID,\n      },\n      system: systemMessage,\n      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n    }).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n (error) => {\n  hasError = true;\n  outputError({\n    errorType: error.name || 'UncaughtException',\n    message: error.message,\n    stack: error.stack,\n  });\n
